title: SD-JWT session token for off-chain proof reuse
description: After a successful handshake, emit an SD-JWT (Selective Disclosure JWT) session token that encodes the verified nullifiers, scope commitment, and expiry. Subsequent API calls within the session present the compact JWT instead of re-running the full ZKP verification. This bridges the gap between ZKP correctness and practical latency requirements: the initial handshake takes ~2s with rapidsnark, but follow-up calls verify in <1ms. The Python SDK already depends on PyJWT; add sd-jwt support to both SDKs with a verifySessionToken() helper.
